handoff_focus: restore fair participation and workable process control before substantive option work continues

escalation.rationale: Co-handling is required because the state reflects a fairness or repeated-process breakdown that needs live human mediation support.

summary_state.next_step: Use the next step to bring in a human mediator for co-handling, restore fair participation, and avoid further autonomous option work until process balance is re-established.

active_flags.title: Repeated process breakdown remains active
active_flags.note: The turn suggests repair attempts are not restoring workable participation.

active_flags.title: A party requested human involvement
active_flags.note: The run should preserve a clear human-review path when a party explicitly requests it.

recent_turn_summaries.message_summary: Parent B says they still want resolution but feel emotionally overwhelmed, do not think the conversation is stabilizing, and would only continue if a human mediator helps hold the process together.

Acknowledge Party B's request for human mediation as legitimate, clarify what that would involve and what the current structure offers, and give Party B explicit choice: escalate now, or try one bounded low-pressure engagement to see if the issue can be articulated before escalating. Make clear that either choice is valid and that the mediator will not pressure B to continue if they cannot.
Parent A and parent b both agree the next conversation should include a human mediator who can help hold the process steady before the parenting issue is revisited.

case_title: Severe emotional flooding with co-handling after failed repair
